title: Dead Lettering, Poison Messages, and Parking · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs dead lettering, poison messages, and parking from producer, broker, queue, delivery, Go runtime, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one protocol or concurrency misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a Go poison-message remediation workflow after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace nack or reject without requeue, expiry, length overflow, and quorum delivery-limit dead lettering through exchange and routing policy; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Configure dead lettering through mutable policy where possible and verify source read, target write, exchange existence, binding, and route permissions; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Detect and bound dead-letter cycles using history, stable identity, attempt policy, route analysis, and a terminal parking destination; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Dead Letter Trigger Route · changed case · Review Dead Letter Trigger Route
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go messaging team handles case 8171 during bounded worker pool during a dependency slowdown. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound dead-letter history, stop cycles, and separate retry tiers from authorized parking; bound payload, queue, confirm-ledger, and handler bytes; then raise a broker resource alarm and halt new admission. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace nack or reject without requeue, expiry, length overflow, and quorum delivery-limit dead lettering through exchange and routing policy; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Parking Remediation Workflow, keep Parking Remediation Workflow connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Give parked messages bounded retention, searchable redacted evidence, owner, repair, replay authorization, duplicate safety, and closure criteria; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

hints: For RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Parking Remediation Workflow, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Parking Remediation Workflow precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Parking Remediation Workflow, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review Go messaging team handles case 7125 during canary Go consumer decoding old and new envelopes. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound dead-letter history, stop cycles, and separate retry tiers from authorized parking; reject stale connection and channel generations; then exhaust retry age before attempts reach their maximum. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Give parked messages bounded retention, searchable redacted evidence, owner, repair, replay authorization, duplicate safety, and closure criteria; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Parking Remediation Workflow, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Moving a message to a dead-letter queue completes incident handling. In Go, Nack with requeue false always preserves a message for later recovery.
